reasoning: The document shows strong content preservation with a fidelity score of 0.8852 and high shingling recall (0.948). Although three minor defects were identified regarding heading hierarchy, list structure, and signature placement, these are cosmetic or structural nuances that do not impede the readability or accuracy of the core content. Per the routing instructions, documents with a fidelity composite >= 0.85 and only minor concerns should be auto-approved.

concerns.analysis: The heading hierarchy in the output is inconsistent with accessibility standards mentioned in the instructions. There is no H1 heading provided in the sections list; the first heading ('North Carolina Department of Health and Human Services...') is assigned H2 level. This creates a skip in the hierarchy. Subsequent headings follow a logical H2 to H3 progression.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: The document starts with agency identification and a formal subject line.
concerns.output_quote: The first heading in the JSON is "level": 2, followed by H2 and H3, but no H1 is present in the document elements.

concerns.analysis: The 'cc' section at the end of the document is a visual list of individuals and roles. In the output JSON, this list is rendered as a single paragraph element with newline characters (\n) separating the items, rather than being structured as a list element with individual list items. This loses the structural semantics of the list.
concerns.source_pdf_quote:

Children’s Program Representatives
Work First Representatives
Family Support and Child Welfare Services Team Leaders
Local Business Liaisons
Sarah Barham
Child Welfare Attorneys

Children’s Program Representatives

Child Welfare Attorneys", ...}]}
